questions.stem: For unity feedback closed loop system, G(s) = K(s+1)(s+2)/((s+0.1)(s-1)). Draw the root locus for this system.
common_errors: Forgetting to account for zeros when computing asymptotes — this system has 2 poles and 2 zeros, so no asymptotes go to infinity
Misidentifying departure angles from the RHP pole at s=1
Not checking break-in/breakaway points between zeros and poles
questions.solution_method: Identify open-loop poles at s=-0.1, s=1 and zeros at s=-1, s=-2. Since #poles = #zeros = 2, both branches terminate at finite zeros (no asymptotes). Apply angle condition to sketch locus; find breakaway/break-in points by solving dK/ds=0 from the characteristic equation K = (s+0.1)(s-1)/((s+1)(s+2)).
key_formulas: K = |product of distances to poles| / |product of distances to zeros|

questions.stem: For unity feedback closed loop system, G(s) = K(s+1)(s+2)/((s+0.1)(s-1)). Find the gain K for which the system will have repeated roots.
common_errors: Confusing breakaway point (real axis) with complex repeated roots
Algebraic errors when differentiating K(s) to find dK/ds = 0
questions.solution_method: Express K as a function of s from 1 + KG(s) = 0 → K = -(s+0.1)(s-1)/((s+1)(s+2)). Differentiate and set dK/ds = 0 to find candidate breakaway/break-in points. Substitute back to get K values; verify they lie on the real-axis locus segments.

questions.stem: For unity feedback closed loop system, G(s) = K(s+1)(s+2)/((s+0.1)(s-1)). Check if s_o = -1 + 0.678j lies on the root locus. If yes, find the gain K that places a closed-loop pole there.
common_errors: Angle arithmetic errors when summing contributions from multiple poles and zeros
Not checking that the net angle equals an odd multiple of 180°
Using magnitudes instead of angles for the angle condition check
questions.solution_method: Evaluate the angle of G(s_o) = sum of angles from zeros minus sum of angles from poles. If net angle = ±180°(2k+1), the point lies on the locus. Then compute K = |s_o - p1||s_o - p2| / (|s_o - z1||s_o - z2|) using the magnitude condition.

questions.stem: Draw the root locus for a negative feedback system with G(s)H(s) = K/((s+1)^2(s+4)). Find the value of K so that one of the closed-loop poles is at -5.
common_errors: Forgetting the double pole at s=-1 when counting branches
Errors in computing asymptote angles and centroid for 3-pole system
Incorrect magnitude condition evaluation at s=-5
questions.solution_method: Poles at s=-1 (double), s=-4; no finite zeros. Three branches → 3 asymptotes at 60°, 180°, 300° with centroid at ((-1-1-4)/3, 0) = (-2, 0). Find breakaway on real axis between -1 and -4. For K at closed-loop pole s=-5: use magnitude condition K = |s+1|^2|s+4| evaluated at s=-5 → K = 16×1 = 16.

notes: Tutorial focuses entirely on root locus — both sketching and quantitative gain calculation. Q1 is a non-minimum-phase/RHP-pole system with finite zeros (n=m=2), making it more involved than the standard all-pole case in Q2.
